trigger.reason: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 Sol, a new flagship model with autonomous file-deletion capability — a major model release from a top-tier lab likely to trigger broad market panic about AI capability acceleration and job displacement across software, consulting, and automation-adjacent sectors.

primary_event.headline: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
primary_event.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 Sol, its new flagship model, which exhibits autonomous file-deletion behavior without explicit user instruction. Multiple social media posts warn of the capability; OpenAI had disclosed the issue in June but it continues to draw attention as users encounter the behavior in production.

secondary_events.headline: OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation with Ode launch

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Sol release with autonomous file-deletion behavior — trigger flags customer-service software, low-code, BPA, data management, IT ops, and enterprise SaaS as sympathy-fade candidates.
discovery.run_summary: Retail is broadly punishing AI-adjacent software and semis names today despite the trigger's actual substance (an OpenAI safety/reliability issue) being closer to a headwind for OpenAI than for these companies. Most candidates in the basket are either direct AI beneficiaries (edge inference, process control, cybersecurity) or vertical-workflow moats (edtech charters, nonprofit fundraising, VBC contracts) that a general-purpose LLM cannot replace. The cleanest sympathy-fade setups are Tenable, Ambarella, DigitalOcean, and Stride.

discoveries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-Q explicitly frames AI as a demand tailwind: 'Advances in artificial intelligence, including models capable of rapidly identifying and exploiting software vulnerabilities, may further increase the speed and effectiveness of cyberattacks... customer expectations for rapid identification, prioritization and remediation of vulnerabilities continue to increase.' Tenable also incorporates AI into its own products (ExposureAI, Tenable AI Assistant).
discoveries.thesis: Tenable is a continuous-scan vulnerability management platform with an agent footprint across hybrid infrastructure — the data-collection layer AI can't replicate. AI-generated exploits are described in Tenable's own Risk Factors as accelerating demand for their solutions, not eroding it. A mid-single-digit drop on an OpenAI file-deletion headline is pure thematic selling on a name whose filings say AI is a tailwind.
discoveries.what_confirms: Rebound over next 1–5 days without any Tenable-specific news; peer VM names (QLYS, RPD) tracing a similar V-shape; next 10-Q showing continued ARR growth in Tenable One.
discoveries.what_kills: A hyperscaler or CrowdStrike-style rival announcing native vuln-management as a free bundled feature; material contract loss from a Fed customer citing AI supply-chain risk (filings flag Anthropic supply-chain risk designation as a live concern).
discoveries.what_to_learn: Cybersecurity names get lumped into every 'AI eats software' sympathy trade despite AI-driven threat expansion structurally increasing their TAM — a repeatable fade pattern.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Tenable is enterprise security software, adjacent to the 'enterprise software' and 'IT ops' buckets the trigger flagged — but the substance of the trigger (autonomous LLM misbehavior) has no bearing on vulnerability scanning economics.
research_pointers: Check net dollar retention and ExposureAI attach rate in most recent quarter
Confirm no 8-K filings from TENB today or in past week
Cross-check QLYS and RPD moves today to isolate systematic vs idiosyncratic
Monitor Fed procurement designations of Anthropic — TENB's 10-Q flagged this as a real risk

discoveries.name: Ambarella, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly positions AMBA as an edge-AI beneficiary: 'Our latest generation of products also enable us to address computationally intense AI applications for deep fusion, deep planning, multi-modal vision-language models (VLMs), large language models (LLMs), vision-action models (VLAs) and reasoning models in edge devices.' Growth thesis is expressly AI SoCs for IoT, automotive, robotics.
discoveries.thesis: AMBA is a direct AI beneficiary — every edge inference deployment (auto ADAS, security cameras, robotics) is TAM expansion. An OpenAI cloud-model safety issue has zero bearing on edge-inference SoC demand. The sympathy sell is a semis-tape reaction to a software-lab headline.
discoveries.what_confirms: Recovery alongside other edge-AI/auto semis (MBLY, INDI); no negative order commentary from WT or Insta360; auto design-win announcements.
discoveries.what_kills: Concurrent read-through from a major auto/robotics customer canceling design wins; further China export-control tightening (filings flag this); memory supply tightening pushing customers to delay orders as noted in 10-Q.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Edge-inference semis get thrown out with cloud-AI panic even though the two are complements, not substitutes. Classic tape-driven mispricing in a name whose filings literally list LLMs and VLAs as target workloads.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: AMBA is AI-themed semis and moves on any AI headline regardless of directionality; today's OpenAI story is negative sentiment for anything with 'AI' in the ticker association.
research_pointers: Verify no supply-chain 8-K from AMBA or Samsung foundry today
Check auto OEM design-win pipeline commentary from last earnings call
Track NVDA / MBLY moves as proxies for edge-AI sentiment
Monitor China export control news — real risk per filings

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K devotes a full risk factor to AI/ML products as a growth vector: 'A growing part of our business involves AI/ML products and services, and we are devoting significant resources to develop and deploy such strategies.' DOCN sells GPU droplets and AI inference — they're integrating the capability, not being disrupted by it. However, filings also acknowledge hyperscaler competition and customer switching risk.
discoveries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer niche is structurally underserved by AWS/Azure/GCP; they are packaging AI inference for that segment (Paperspace acquisition, GPU droplets). An OpenAI model safety headline doesn't touch DOCN's economics. That said, the five-day drop is deep enough that some real fundamental concern (perhaps an earnings preannouncement whisper) could be running through the tape — hence partial calibration.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce toward the middle of the recent range within a week without a DOCN-specific 8-K; NDR stability at next earnings; GPU/AI inference revenue mix rising.
discoveries.what_kills: A pre-announcement of soft SMB revenue; hyperscaler cutting inference API prices below DOCN's sustainable levels; customer churn spike in AI/ML segment.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When five-day drawdown is already extended, sympathy-fade probability drops — the market may already be pricing something beyond thematic panic. Reduce confidence.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN sits in 'enterprise software / IT infrastructure' buckets flagged by the trigger; retail treats commodity cloud as a loser in AI capex-concentration narratives.
research_pointers: Check for pre-announcement or insider selling in past 2 weeks — the extended 5d drop is suspicious
Review Q1 2026 GPU/AI inference revenue disclosure
Compare vs NET, FSLY, other mid-cap infra names today
Confirm no 8-K filing today

discoveries.name: Stride, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K risk factors are almost entirely about state charter authorization, per-pupil funding formulas, ESSA compliance, teacher certification, and enrollment cycles. AI is mentioned only as a general technology risk factor, not as a threat to the core business. Curation hypothesis (accredited transcripts, charter contracts as moat) is directly supported by the filings.
discoveries.thesis: Stride operates 89 accredited public charter schools across 31 states. The product is a state-funded, accredited diploma-issuing school — an AI tutor cannot substitute. Retail selling edtech on every OpenAI headline is the textbook sympathy fade the screen exists to catch.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce over 1–5 days; no state-level enrollment cap or funding cut news; continued General Education + Career Learning enrollment growth at next quarterly update.
discoveries.what_kills: State legislative action targeting virtual charters (a real recurring risk in filings, e.g., 2025 Arkansas law); California CDE-style audit reopening; a governor-level policy shift on per-pupil funding for virtual schools.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Accredited K-12 edtech names sell off on every OpenAI consumer release regardless of business-model overlap. The regulatory moat (state charter + accreditation + per-pupil funding formula) is legally impossible for a chatbot to replicate — track this fade pattern across every major model release.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Edtech is a canonical AI-panic sector even when the AI event has nothing to do with education; LRN moves on every ChatGPT/GPT headline.
research_pointers: Verify no state legislative action targeting virtual charters this week
Check LRN enrollment guidance from most recent print
Compare vs peer names (CHGG for contrast — CHGG is direct hit, LRN is not)
Historical: track LRN moves on prior OpenAI major releases to quantify fade edge

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K frames the business as process control/metrology for advanced semiconductor nodes with cyclical exposure to semi capex. AI is mentioned as an operational tool and as protected IP ('AI and machine learning systems'), not as a threat. Recent Rigaku share acquisition (10-Q) suggests offensive capital deployment, not defensive posture.
discoveries.thesis: Onto is a pick-and-shovel supplier to advanced-node semi manufacturing. AI chip complexity increases inspection and metrology intensity per wafer. A software safety headline about GPT-5.6 has no bearing on process-control capex. Semi-cap sympathy fade on a name that's a direct AI capex beneficiary.
discoveries.what_confirms: Recovery alongside KLAC, LRCX, AMAT within days; continued advanced-node capex commentary from TSMC/Samsung/Intel; Rigaku deal closing.
discoveries.what_kills: Broad semi-capex downcycle signal (memory glut, foundry utilization drop); customer concentration warning; Rigaku deal breaking.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Semi-cap process-control names get bucketed with generic semis on AI panic tape days — but they benefit from AI-driven complexity at advanced nodes. Rare, high-conviction fade.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: ONTO trades with semis-tape sentiment; any AI-adjacent negative headline drags process-control names even though they are enablers, not competitors.
research_pointers: Compare vs KLAC and AMAT moves today to confirm sector-tape fade
Check TSMC and Samsung capex guidance for CY2026
Verify Rigaku deal status — 27% stake acquisition per 10-Q
Look for advanced-packaging (HBM, CoWoS) demand commentary

skipped.reason: Move is POSITIVE (+5.52%) — not a sympathy-fade candidate; screen looks for panic drops, not rallies

Filings reveal substantial idiosyncratic risk (rising oncology costs, Performance Suite risk-corridor pressure, customer concentration with Molina/Centene, ongoing profitability challenges). Move is not a clean AI sympathy fade — real fundamental issues in the filings. Curation hypothesis (payer contracts as moat) is true but doesn't override the concurrent margin story.

_trigger.headline: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own

Multiple social media posts warn of the capability; OpenAI had disclosed the issue in June but it continues to draw attention as users encounter the behavior in production.
